name: brass mechanical beetle
shortDescription: A tiny brass beetle with jointed legs, jeweled rivets, and a keyhole tucked under one wing. It is sold as a curiosity, a desk toy, and proof that clockmakers sometimes have more patience than sense.
flavorText: “Wind it once for charm, twice for conversation, and never three times indoors.”

name: glass apple
shortDescription: A clear glass apple shaped with careful weight and a cool shine. Teachers, healers, nobles, and collectors prize it because it looks like fruit and survives like furniture.
flavorText: “The orchard that grew this had no roots, only a steady hand.”

name: memory ribbon
shortDescription: A soft ribbon tied with tiny knots for promises, debts, names, and roads that should not be forgotten. Careful merchants use it when their ledgers are elsewhere and trust is too expensive.
flavorText: “Tie it before you swear, untie it after you pay.”

name: soft kid leather
shortDescription: A supple piece of young goat leather prized for gloves, pouches, book covers, and fine lining work. It takes dye cleanly and sells best to artisans who judge by touch first.
flavorText: “Some leather is bought by the sheet. This is bought by the fingertips.”

name: thick oxhide
shortDescription: Heavy oxhide cut for straps, shields, soles, and work aprons that must endure honest punishment. It is not elegant, but it survives rain, road dust, and bad tempers.
flavorText: “Beauty cracks. Oxhide argues back.”

name: dyed belt leather
shortDescription: A dressed strip of belt leather already dyed for buckles, pouches, harness repairs, or travelers who need sturdier closures. The color adds shelf appeal without making it too precious for use.
flavorText: “A belt is only noticed when it fails.”

name: leather cord spool
shortDescription: A spool of narrow leather cord for lacing armor, binding parcels, repairing tack, and tying shut anything that should not open on the road. Small stock, but always useful.
flavorText: “The cheapest thing in the pack may be what keeps the pack together.”

name: bridle ring set
shortDescription: A matched set of metal bridle rings for horse tack, cart harnesses, and stable repairs. Buyers inspect their weight and smoothness, since rough rings ruin good leather quickly.
flavorText: “A rider praises the horse. The stablemaster checks the rings.”

name: saddle strap bundle
shortDescription: A bundle of cut saddle straps, oiled and sorted by length for quick repairs. Caravans buy them before bad roads, wise riders before leaving, and fools after walking home.
flavorText: “No horse ever apologized for breaking a strap.”

name: waxed satchel flap
shortDescription: A waxed leather satchel flap made to shed rain and close over travel papers or coin pouches. It looks dull on a dry day and priceless during a storm.
flavorText: “Dry papers make calmer merchants.”

name: hunter pelt roll
shortDescription: A rolled stack of cleaned pelts from a hunter’s winter catch, tied tight for transport. Furriers judge softness and scent, while frontier traders judge how quickly it becomes warmth.
flavorText: “The forest keeps its accounts in tooth marks and cold nights.”

name: fur trim strip
shortDescription: A narrow strip of fur prepared for collars, cuffs, hoods, and decorative edging. It is small enough for modest buyers but fine enough to make plain clothing look deliberate.
flavorText: “A little fur can make last year’s coat pretend it is new.”

name: cobbler sole blanks
shortDescription: Thick sole blanks cut for cobblers who would rather fit shoes than saw raw hide. They sell steadily wherever roads chew boots faster than wages rise.
flavorText: “Every journey ends at the soles.”

name: tin nugget pouch
shortDescription: A small pouch of tin nuggets for alloy work, soldering, and modest casting jobs. The metal is humble, which is why workshops miss it the moment it runs out.
flavorText: “Tin never boasts. Bronze does that for it.”

name: copper nugget pouch
shortDescription: A pouch of copper nuggets with a warm red shine and a faint mineral smell. Jewelers, smiths, and tinkers all find uses for it, keeping its market steady.
flavorText: “Copper is the color of small profit done often.”

name: silver ore chips
shortDescription: A cloth packet of silver-bearing ore chips, dull at first glance but promising under a knowledgeable eye. Assayers weigh the stone while miners sell the hope.
flavorText: “Ore is a promise that charges by the pound.”

name: gold dust vial
shortDescription: A sealed vial of fine gold dust, bright enough to attract attention and troublesome enough to require honest scales. Gilders, alchemists, and luxury pigment makers all ask after it.
flavorText: “Gold dust escapes like gossip and settles like debt.”

name: coal sack
shortDescription: A soot-marked sack of coal for forges, hearths, kilns, and cold warehouses. It is dirty stock with clean value whenever fuel runs short.
flavorText: “Nobody admires coal until the fire goes out.”

name: riverstone ore pan
shortDescription: A shallow pan containing river stones and promising flecks gathered from a cold streambed. Prospectors call it opportunity; careful merchants call it something to assay first.
flavorText: “The river gives nothing away. It only lets you carry questions downstream.”

name: blueglass ore sample
shortDescription: A blue-tinted ore sample with glassy veins that catch light like frozen harbor water. Alchemists and glassmakers both ask questions about it, which is usually good for the price.
flavorText: “Some stones are mined. Others audition.”

name: deepforge sample box
shortDescription: A boxed set of dark ore fragments from a deep forge claim, labeled by seam and depth. It is assay stock, bragging piece, and investment bait in one tidy container.
flavorText: “Every mine sells two things first: samples and confidence.”

name: laying hen
shortDescription: A reliable laying hen, more valuable for tomorrow’s egg than today’s stew. Farmers judge her by comb, weight, and temper; innkeepers ask whether she is still working.
flavorText: “A small bird that pays rent one shell at a time.”

name: duck pair
shortDescription: A paired set of ducks sold for eggs, feathers, pest control, and noisy charm. They travel poorly but settle well, provided the buyer has water and tolerance.
flavorText: “Buy one duck and you own a bird. Buy two and you own an argument.”

name: goose
shortDescription: A watchful goose with a loud complaint for every stranger and a strong sense of property. It is useful for eggs, feathers, and alarming thieves before the dogs wake.
flavorText: “A goose guards with no wages and too much enthusiasm.”

name: milk goat
shortDescription: A milk goat fit for a small yard, caravan camp, or hillside farm. She offers steady value in milk, cheese, and the mild destruction of anything left within reach.
flavorText: “Cheaper than a cow, cleverer than a fence.”

name: wool sheep
shortDescription: A wool sheep valued for fleece, breeding, and quiet pasture profit. The best buyers look past the wool to the hooves, teeth, and weather sense.
flavorText: “A sheep is a walking blanket with opinions about gates.”

name: pony
shortDescription: A sturdy pony suited for carts, children, short riders, and difficult paths where grand horses become expensive mistakes. It lacks glamour, not usefulness.
flavorText: “Pride rides tall. Trade rides what keeps moving.”

name: ox
shortDescription: A strong ox for pulling carts, plows, sledges, and the fortunes of people who own heavier things than they can lift. Slow, patient, and worth feeding properly.
flavorText: “The ox never hurries, which is why it finishes.”

name: feed trough
shortDescription: A solid feed trough built for stable yards, pens, and caravan animals. It is plain carpentry, but plain carpentry becomes valuable when hungry animals stop wasting grain.
flavorText: “Good feeding begins before the feed is poured.”

name: veterinary salve
shortDescription: A thick veterinary salve used on cracked hooves, rubbed harness marks, and small stable injuries. Animal handlers buy it by smell, reputation, and how calmly it spreads.
flavorText: “Beasts forgive many things, but not neglect.”

name: apple cider jug
shortDescription: A jug of apple cider, sweet-sharp and cloudy from honest pressing. It sells well at harvest fairs, roadside inns, and anywhere thirsty laborers want something brighter than water.
flavorText: “Orchards travel best in jugs.”

name: dark malt barrel
shortDescription: A barrel of dark malt with roasted depth for brewers who want body, color, and a little smoke in the cup. Tavern owners call it future profit with hoops.
flavorText: “The barrel is quiet because the flavor is still thinking.”

name: sailor grog bottle
shortDescription: A stout bottle of sailor grog, rough, warming, and built for wet evenings near the docks. It is not refined, but it understands its audience perfectly.
flavorText: “A civilized drink would not survive the pier.”

name: moonmilk crock
shortDescription: A sealed crock of pale moonmilk, a delicate dairy good sold for kitchens, remedies, and night-market sweets. Buyers check the seal first and the story second.
flavorText: “Milk the color of midnight pretending to be dawn.”

name: coffee bean sack
shortDescription: A sack of roasted coffee beans with a bitter perfume that wakes a room before the kettle boils. Scholars, dock clerks, caravan guards, and ambitious bakers all pay attention.
flavorText: “The bean that turns dawn from an accusation into a schedule.”

name: cocoa nib pouch
shortDescription: A pouch of cocoa nibs, dark, fragrant, and costly enough to measure carefully. Confectioners prize them, nobles request them, and smugglers call them medicinal.
flavorText: “Bitter first, valuable second, addictive by the third trade.”

name: tap spigot
shortDescription: A metal tap spigot for barrels of cider, ale, oil, or anything else that should leave slowly instead of all at once. Tavern owners regret not stocking extras.
flavorText: “A barrel without a tap is just a heavy secret.”

name: warehouse claim ticket
shortDescription: A stamped warehouse claim ticket naming a stored lot, shelf, or sealed crate. It is light as paper but can be worth a wagonload if the clerk honors the ink.
flavorText: “The ticket is smaller than the cargo and somehow easier to lose.”

name: customs tag
shortDescription: A customs tag marked with inspection notes, duty stamps, and a string worn from travel. Honest merchants keep them visible; clever ones keep several.
flavorText: “A tag can open a gate faster than a smile.”

name: import permit
shortDescription: An import permit with official script, countersigns, and enough blank space to worry a careful trader. It turns forbidden cargo into taxable cargo, a profitable miracle.
flavorText: “Law is sometimes just ink standing between you and a fine.”

name: apprentice papers
shortDescription: A folded set of apprentice papers naming a trade, master, and term of service. Guilds inspect them closely, parents save them proudly, and apprentices notice the end date.
flavorText: “A young craftsperson’s first tool is permission.”

name: blank contract
shortDescription: A clean blank contract on decent paper, ready for terms, signatures, seals, and later disappointment. Merchants value it because an empty promise is still easier to sell than a broken one.
flavorText: “All fortunes begin as blank spaces someone was brave enough to fill.”

name: tax seal strip
shortDescription: A strip of official tax seals used to mark paid duties, cleared cargo, or goods that have already suffered bureaucracy. Useful, fragile, and always pricier than it looks.
flavorText: “Nothing says freedom like proof of payment.”

name: witness ribbon
shortDescription: A witness ribbon meant to bind signed papers, sworn statements, or sealed agreements in front of respectable eyes. Its value lies in the trouble it creates for liars.
flavorText: “Tie the ribbon where memory may be tempted to wander.”

name: boundary map
shortDescription: A boundary map showing fields, ditches, stones, lanes, and disputed lines that probably looked clearer before neighbors got involved. Land agents and lawyers both find it useful.
flavorText: “A border is a line on paper until someone plants beans across it.”

name: small drum
shortDescription: A small hand drum with taut skin and a carrying cord, suitable for signals, songs, market calls, or children with forgiving neighbors. Its tone is simple but travels well.
flavorText: “Small drums make large announcements.”

name: brass bell chime
shortDescription: A brass bell chime with a clear bright note, used for shops, shrines, ships, and doorways where arrival should be prettier than shouting. Tidy establishments love it.
flavorText: “A bell is manners made of metal.”

name: song scroll
shortDescription: A song scroll written with lyrics, marks, and performance notes for a tune that has traveled farther than its composer. Bards buy it and taverns copy it badly.
flavorText: “No road is long enough to keep a good chorus buried.”

name: music box cylinder
shortDescription: A pinned music box cylinder that plays only when matched to the right mechanism. Collectors love the promise of it; repairers love charging to discover the tune.
flavorText: “A song trapped in brass, waiting for the correct little prison.”

name: shell rattle
shortDescription: A rattle made from small shells tied with cord and polished by hand. It serves music, ritual, children, and seaside sellers who understand the value of sound in a stall.
flavorText: “The shore keeps rhythm even after it leaves the water.”

name: ship bell
shortDescription: A heavy ship bell with a weathered rim and a voice built to cut through fog, rain, and panic. Harbor buyers respect it if the tone is clean and the mounting sound.
flavorText: “On a ship, time is not read. It is struck.”

name: anchor charm
shortDescription: A small anchor charm worn for luck, safe return, and stubborn hearts. Sailors buy them for voyages, lovers for promises, and jewelers for easy symbolism.
flavorText: “Stay, says the charm. Sail, says the tide.”

name: rope hawser
shortDescription: A thick rope hawser made for mooring, hauling, and other work where weak cord becomes comedy. It smells of tar, salt, and practical decisions.
flavorText: “A good rope is only praised after the bad one snaps.”

name: fishing float bundle
shortDescription: A bundle of fishing floats, corked, painted, and ready for nets or lines. Cheap by the piece, useful by the dozen, and easy to sell wherever water smells like work.
flavorText: “Small floats mark where patience has been thrown.”

name: eel basket
shortDescription: A narrow eel basket woven to let water pass while discouraging escape. Fisherfolk inspect the mouth first, because eels have more ambition than gratitude.
flavorText: “If it can wriggle, the basket must be smarter.”

name: net weight pouch
shortDescription: A pouch of small net weights for fishing gear, seine lines, and careful repairs. They are dull until the first good catch, after which everyone remembers who stocked them.
flavorText: “The net rises because the weights know when to sink.”

name: captain logbook
shortDescription: A captain logbook filled with dated entries, weather notes, cargo marks, and the omissions that make insurers squint. Even a dull voyage can become valuable in writing.
flavorText: “The sea forgets quickly. Captains are paid not to.”

name: deck brush
shortDescription: A stiff deck brush for scrubbing planks, stalls, kitchens, and any floor that has seen too much traffic. It is honest work stock and never stays clean long.
flavorText: “Some tools are born knowing they will be blamed for dirt.”

name: consecrated salt
shortDescription: A pouch of consecrated salt blessed for thresholds, shrines, preservation, and uneasy travelers. Whether one believes or not, it sells best when the seal is unbroken.
flavorText: “Salt keeps meat, roads, and certain fears from spoiling.”

name: painted prayer bead
shortDescription: A single painted prayer bead, smooth from handling and bright with patient brushwork. Pilgrims buy them as replacements, children as treasures, and priests as small mercies.
flavorText: “One bead is not a prayer, but it remembers where the prayer began.”

name: small brass idol
shortDescription: A small brass idol worn smooth at the edges by hands, pockets, and offerings. It fits a shelf, shrine, or travel pouch, carrying more devotion than metal weight.
flavorText: “Little gods travel best.”

name: festival wreath
shortDescription: A festival wreath woven with flowers, ribbons, and seasonal greenery. It is temporary by nature, which makes buyers surprisingly willing to pay for freshness and color.
flavorText: “Celebration is often just beauty with a short shelf life.”

name: memorial card
shortDescription: A memorial card printed on fine stock with a name, dates, and a border meant to be handled gently. It is small, solemn, and difficult to haggle over without looking cruel.
flavorText: “Some paper is kept because it says what people cannot.”

name: silvered prayer pin
shortDescription: A silvered prayer pin made to fasten cloth, mark devotion, or carry a blessing close to the heart. The silvering is thin, but buyers rarely attach thin meaning to it.
flavorText: “A little shine for faith, grief, and safe roads.”
